user_query:
I run a small community indie film discussion group focused on mental health themes. Using the local inputs at input/screenings.csv, input/contacts.json, input/preferences.yaml, and the draft message at input/email_template.md, please do the following:

1) Build a reminder and follow-up schedule as a structured CSV at outputs/reminder_schedule.csv. Include only film-contact pairs that satisfy preferences in input/preferences.yaml: mental_health_theme must be one of the allowed themes, film_rating must meet or exceed the minimum, and any film whose tags include an excluded tag (e.g., "horror") must be filtered out. For each remaining film-contact pair, create two rows: one for a reminder and one for a follow-up.

- Fields required per row: contact_email, contact_name, film_title, screening_datetime (ISO 8601 in the screening timezone), action ("reminder" or "follow_up"), send_on (ISO 8601 in the contact’s timezone with time set to 10:00), priority_score (numeric), reason (short text summarizing top factors used in ranking), template_path (the path to the template to use).
- Compute send_on deterministically: reminder send_on = screening_date minus notify_window_days (from the contact) at 10:00 local time; follow_up send_on = screening_date plus follow_up_window_days at 10:00 local time.
- Compute priority_score using these weights from input/preferences.yaml and this formula: priority_score = film_rating_weight * (film_rating/10) + alignment_weight * alignment + engagement_weight * engagement_score + awards_bonus, where awards_bonus is mapped from awards (winner/nominee/none) per preferences. Compute alignment as the maximum of: 1.0 if the film’s mental_health_theme exactly matches any contact interest_tags; else 0.8 if any film tag overlaps contact interest_tags; else 0.5 if the film’s mental_health_theme is allowed but has no tag overlap; else 0.0.
- Sort outputs/reminder_schedule.csv by send_on ascending, then by priority_score descending.
- Set template_path to outputs/updated_email_template.md for all rows.

2) Edit and rewrite the draft message at input/email_template.md into outputs/updated_email_template.md, preserving the placeholders {name}, {film_title}, {date}, {time}, {venue}, and {theme}. Make the message concise and warm, add a clear call to RSVP, and include a brief boundary note consistent with my role as a psychologist (e.g., this is a discussion group, not therapy, and personal clinical details shouldn’t be shared).

3) Implement a runnable script at scripts/generate_messages.py that reads outputs/reminder_schedule.csv and outputs/updated_email_template.md, fills placeholders for each row, and writes per-message files under outputs/messages/<contact_email>/<action>_<film_title>.md. The script must also produce a run log at outputs/logs/run_<YYYYMMDDHHMM>.txt that includes a summary count of messages generated per action (reminder vs follow_up) and the full paths of all created files.

4) Run scripts/generate_messages.py once so the message previews and the run log are actually created in the workspace.

Everything should be self-contained and reproducible from the provided inputs. Please make sure all four required operations are essential to completing this request. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
